mnemonics: Adders Add, MUXes Multiplex (like a TV selector)
DEMUX is the reverse of a MUX (De-Multiplexes one input to many outputs)
K-Maps: Group Ones for SOP, Zeros for POS (SOP = Sum of Products, POS = Product of Sums)
keyPoints: A combinational circuit's output depends ONLY on the current input combination.
Basic building blocks include Adders, Subtractors, Multiplexers (MUX), Demultiplexers (DEMUX), and Decoders.
Boolean algebra and Karnaugh Maps (K-Maps) are used to simplify and design these circuits.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that combinational circuits have no memory (no feedback loops).
Incorrectly grouping cells in a K-Map, leading to a non-minimal simplified expression.
Confusing a Decoder (input selects an output) with a Demultiplexer (input and select channel data to an output).
